deep_dive.executive_summary: At $19.27, the base case is 2.2% per year over seven years on normalized owner cash of $0.35 per share. Firefly is a national-security-weighted launch and spacecraft company with $652.6 million of contracted backlog and milestone billing that typically collects roughly 90% of contract value before launch. Revenue is growing ($80.9 million in Q1 2026), but the company still lost $96.7 million in the quarter as research and development and SciTec integration costs ran ahead of gross profit ($17.5 million, 21.6% margin). Cash is $326 million after the SciTec deal. Moat is unproven, dhando is p...

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: Predictable cash-flow security
applicability_tests: Incremental capital can be tied to identifiable projects, cohorts, or contracted capacity.
Returns on new capital can be separated from returns on the installed base.
Contract duration, volume protection, pricing, and counterparty risk are observable.
Leverage and future owner-funded capital are explicitly deducted.
failure_modes: contract renewal or regulatory reset ignored

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: A complete security value is intentionally withheld while material components are unpriced. Attribution restarts after the first proof-complete baseline.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Operating-only fallback. Add an explicit component_valuation schedule before drawing asset-level conclusions.

valuation_proof.adjustment: Risk and timing are reflected in the low/base/high range.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Owner cash, reinvestment economics, or competitive position remains below the low-case path for two reporting periods.

timeline.label: Q1 2026 net loss $96.7M on revenue $80.9M (+45% YoY); cash $326M after SciTec integration spend
FY2025 revenue $159.9M (+163% YoY); OCF $204.9M; RPO and deferred revenue build on milestone billing
Closed SciTec acquisition — defense software, classified infrastructure, ~$652M purchase price allocation assets
Northrop Grumman partnership on Eclipse medium-lift vehicle; $50M equity investment disclosed
Nasdaq listing (FLY) following IPO / de-SPAC; expanded public float and reporting cadence
Blue Ghost lunar lander program advances; NASA CLPS and national security mission pipeline cited in 10-K
Spaceflight Inc. combination adds on-orbit services capability (disclosed in 10-K segment history)
Alpha rocket achieves orbit — company cites only U.S. small-lift provider with demonstrated orbit in 1,000 kg class

industry.structure: U.S. national-security-led launch and space services oligopoly forming among a handful of qualified primes and new-space entrants; customer concentration toward NASA, Space Force, and prime contractors.
industry.share_shift: Firefly gaining share in small-lift and lunar delivery via Alpha and Blue Ghost wins; medium-lift share depends on Eclipse delivery versus ULA, SpaceX, and Rocket Lab.
industry.trend: Shift to firm-fixed-price defense contracts, higher launch cadence demand, and integrated launch-plus-spacecraft-plus-software stacks (SciTec acquisition).

At **$19.27** per share, we expect about **2.2%** per year over **7 years** on the base case (normalized free cash flow of **$0.35** per share).

risk_claims.claim: AEROEQUITY GP, LLC reported an insider sale of 8,000,000 shares ($384,000,000).

*Primary risk: Launch failure or Eclipse development delay collides with $67.5 million quarterly research and development spend, turning $652.6 million of backlog into cash burn rather than owner cash.

*Lens failure mode (operating): Treating $652.6 million of RPO (~$4.08 per share) as net asset value, or using FY2025 operating cash flow $204.9 million as sustainable equity free cash flow without subtracting growth capital spending, integration costs, and launch failure probability.
